intent_raw: re-release arbiter on github using gh cli as GPLv3 (license migration)
intent_reframed: Migrate the arbiter / mcp-proxy-firewall repository from Apache-2.0 to GPL-3.0-or-later for all code authored by Veronica Burnz, update all license declarations (LICENSE file, workspace + per-crate Cargo.toml `license` fields, README badge and License section), commit on a dedicated branch off main, open and merge a PR, tag a new semver-bumped release (v0.1.0 proposed — reserve v0.0.x semantics for Apache-2.0 lineage), and publish it via `gh release create` with release notes that explicitly call out the license change and that the v0.0.11 release remains Apache-2.0 licensed in perpetuity.
surfaced_assumptions: A0-COPYRIGHT: All code in the repo is authored solely by Veronica Burnz. VERIFIED via `git log` unique authors — all four identities resolve to the same person. No third-party contributors to relicense around.

A1-TARGET: 'GPLv3' means GPL-3.0-or-later (the `or later` variant) unless the user explicitly wants GPL-3.0-only. Default to -or-later for ecosystem flexibility; flag for confirmation if Pass 0 artifact lands in interactive mode. In autonomous mode: default to GPL-3.0-or-later.

A2-RERELEASE: 'Re-release' means publish a NEW tag/release under GPL-3.0 — NOT retroactively change v0.0.11. The existing v0.0.11 artifact was published under Apache-2.0 and legally remains so in perpetuity. Migration applies to HEAD and all future releases.
A3-GHCLI: `gh release create` is the publishing mechanism; not `cargo publish`, not `gh repo edit`. Crates.io is out of scope unless user later invokes it.

A4-REPO: The repository is `vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall` on GitHub. Cargo.toml's `repository` field currently points to a stale URL `github.com/vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall`. Fix this drift as part of the migration, since touching Cargo.toml anyway.

A5-DEPENDENCIES: GPL-3.0 requires all statically-linked dependencies to be GPL-compatible. Cargo.lock must be audited: any MPL-2.0, LGPL, GPL-2.0-only, or CDDL dependency is a blocker. This audit is a hard gate in Phase 1.
A6-BRANCH: Current branch `fix/external-lens-audit-2026-04-12` is unrelated; license migration lands on a fresh `license/gpl3-migration` branch cut from `origin/main`.

unpacked_terms.re-release: cut a new git tag, create a GitHub Release via `gh release create`, attach notes — NOT overwrite v0.0.11
unpacked_terms.arbiter: the project name; the repo is `mcp-proxy-firewall` on GitHub but crates are `arbiter-*`. Treat 'arbiter' as the user's canonical project name.
unpacked_terms.gh cli: `gh release create <tag> --title ... --notes-file ...` — NOT the web UI, NOT `cargo publish`
unpacked_terms.license migration: LICENSE file replacement + all metadata declarations updated + README prose updated + commit trail preserves the prior Apache-2.0 LICENSE in git history

criteria.name: Copyright ownership verified
criteria.description: Confirm no external contributors exist whose code cannot be unilaterally relicensed. Check `git log --format='%ae' | sort -u`; all identities must resolve to the sole copyright holder.
criteria.threshold: one human identity across all commits

criteria.name: Dependency license compatibility
criteria.description: All runtime dependencies in Cargo.lock must be GPL-3.0-compatible. Run `cargo tree` or license-check tooling; flag any MPL-2.0, LGPL-2.1, GPL-2.0-only, CDDL, EPL, or proprietary licenses.
criteria.threshold: zero incompatible licenses OR documented isolation strategy

criteria.name: gh CLI authenticated
criteria.description: `gh auth status` reports working auth. Currently blocked by invalid GITHUB_TOKEN env var overriding keyring token. Resolve by unsetting in the execution shell.

criteria.name: LICENSE file replaced
criteria.description: LICENSE file contains the full GPL-3.0 text verbatim from gnu.org. Git history retains the Apache-2.0 LICENSE (do not rewrite history).
criteria.threshold: diff shows Apache-2.0 removed, GPL-3.0 added in one commit

criteria.name: Cargo manifests updated
criteria.description: Workspace Cargo.toml `license` field = `GPL-3.0-or-later`. All 14 crate-level Cargo.toml files inherit via `license.workspace = true` OR declare `license = "GPL-3.0-or-later"` directly. Workspace `repository` field corrected to `https://github.com/vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall`.
criteria.threshold: `grep -r Apache Cargo.toml crates/*/Cargo.toml` returns zero matches

criteria.name: README updated
criteria.description: Three sites: shield badge, prose mention (line 67-69), License section (line 235). Update all to GPL-3.0. Add a short 'License history' subsection noting v0.0.11 and earlier are Apache-2.0.
criteria.threshold: zero 'Apache' matches outside a clearly-labeled history note

criteria.name: Migration committed and reviewed
criteria.description: Changes live on `license/gpl3-migration` branch, committed with a clear commit message citing the license change. Merged to `main` via PR (even if auto-merged by the same user — preserves audit trail).
criteria.threshold: branch pushed, PR opened and merged, `main` contains the migration commit

criteria.name: Version bumped
criteria.description: Workspace version bumped from 0.0.11. Proposed: 0.1.0 (signals breaking licensing change per the 'license is interface' reading of semver). Documented in CHANGELOG or release notes.

criteria.name: GitHub Release published
criteria.description: `gh release create v0.1.0` succeeds. Release notes explicitly call out: (a) project is now GPL-3.0-or-later; (b) v0.0.11 and prior remain Apache-2.0; (c) contributor guidelines implicitly change (inbound=outbound, so PRs now inbound-licensed GPL-3.0).
criteria.threshold: `gh release view v0.1.0` returns published state with notes present

criteria.name: Post-publish verification
criteria.description: GitHub repo 'License' sidebar (auto-detected by GitHub's licensee tool) shows GPL-3.0. `gh api repos/vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall --jq .license.key` returns `gpl-3.0`.
criteria.threshold: API reports gpl-3.0; may need up to 60s for propagation

tensions.name: Irreversibility vs autonomous speed
tensions.description: Autonomous mode pressures for uninterrupted execution. License publication is irreversible — once tagged and pushed, forks can propagate. Tension resolves by treating Phase 3 (publish) as a hard verification gate: Phase 2 must fully converge before Phase 3 runs. Do NOT collapse phases.
tensions.disposition: preserve as structural constraint — phase boundary is load-bearing

tensions.name: Copyleft strictness vs ecosystem breadth
tensions.a: User's explicit GPL-3.0 choice (signals copyleft intent)
tensions.b: Apache-2.0 permissiveness (current state, broader adoption surface)
tensions.description: Not the compiler's to resolve. User has chosen. Flag only so /execute --interrogate does not second-guess the choice.

tensions.name: Naming drift (adjacent to migration, not part of it)
tensions.description: Pre-existing drift surfaced by interrogation. Fixing it is in-scope because Cargo.toml is being touched anyway. Do not expand to repo rename — out of scope.
tensions.disposition: fold into C-005; defer any repo rename

tensions.name: Version bump semantics
tensions.a: License change is a breaking change for downstream consumers
tensions.b: semver reserves major bumps for API changes, not legal changes
tensions.description: There is no canonical semver ruling for license changes. Choosing 0.1.0 (not 1.0.0) signals 'breaking-ish but still pre-1.0' and is defensible. Flag in release notes so consumers know to audit.
tensions.disposition: resolve to 0.1.0; document rationale in release notes

tensions.name: Inbound contributions license
tensions.a: Prior contributors (none external, but future PRs)
tensions.description: After migration, inbound=outbound convention means PRs are implicitly GPL-3.0-licensed. Worth a CONTRIBUTING.md note but out of scope for this migration unless a CONTRIBUTING file already exists.
tensions.disposition: note in release; check CONTRIBUTING.md existence in Phase 1, add line if present

steps.name: Verify sole copyright
steps.action: Run `git log --format='%ae' | sort -u`. Confirm all emails map to the single copyright holder. If any external email appears, HALT and write to BLOCKED.md.

steps.name: Audit dependency licenses
steps.action: Run `cargo tree --format '{p} {l}' 2>/dev/null` OR install `cargo-license` and run it. Filter output for licenses incompatible with GPL-3.0: CDDL, EPL, GPL-2.0-only, proprietary. MPL-2.0 is compatible when kept as a separate module. Apache-2.0, MIT, BSD, ISC, Unlicense, CC0 are all GPL-3-compatible.

steps.name: Fix gh auth
steps.action: Diagnose via `gh auth status`. If GITHUB_TOKEN env var is the blocker, document that the execution shell must `unset GITHUB_TOKEN` before any `gh` invocation. Cannot modify parent shell; emit a preamble step for Phase 3.

steps.name: Confirm main is the base
steps.action: `git fetch origin && git log origin/main..HEAD` on current branch to confirm the active branch does not contain changes that must land on main before license migration. Plan to branch off `origin/main` directly.

steps.name: Create migration branch
steps.action: `git fetch origin && git checkout -b license/gpl3-migration origin/main`. Do NOT branch from the current fix/external-lens-audit branch.

steps.name: Replace LICENSE with GPL-3.0 text
steps.action: Fetch canonical GPL-3.0 text from https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt OR use `curl -sL https://www.gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.txt -o LICENSE`. Verify byte count and SHA-256 against an expected value if available; otherwise verify first/last lines match canonical.

steps.name: Update workspace Cargo.toml
steps.action: Edit the root Cargo.toml: set `license = "GPL-3.0-or-later"`, bump `version = "0.1.0"`, correct `repository = "https://github.com/vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall"`, correct `homepage` if also stale.

steps.name: Propagate to crate manifests
steps.action: For each of the 14 crates/*/Cargo.toml: verify it uses `license.workspace = true` OR has an explicit `license =`. If explicit, change to `GPL-3.0-or-later`. Prefer `license.workspace = true` where feasible.

steps.name: Update README.md
steps.action: Three edits: (1) badge URL + alt text → GPL-3.0 shield, (2) line ~69 prose → 'GPL-3.0-or-later', (3) License section → link to LICENSE with GPL-3.0 title. Add a one-line history note: 'Releases v0.0.11 and earlier were published under Apache-2.0.'

steps.name: Sanity checks
steps.action: (a) `cargo check --workspace` compiles. (b) `grep -rn Apache Cargo.toml crates/*/Cargo.toml README.md` returns only history-note matches. (c) `cargo metadata --format-version 1 --no-deps | jq '.packages[].license' | sort -u` returns only GPL-3.0-or-later.

steps.name: Commit and push branch
steps.action: `git add -A` (license+manifests+README, NOT property_tests.proptest-regressions or diff.diff — exclude those explicitly), commit with message 'License migration: Apache-2.0 → GPL-3.0-or-later' + body explaining v0.0.11-and-earlier retain Apache-2.0. Push to origin.

steps.name: Open and merge PR
steps.action: `gh pr create --base main --title ... --body ...`. In autonomous mode, merge immediately via `gh pr merge --squash --delete-branch` once CI green (or without wait if no CI gates). Do NOT force-push.
steps.gate: PR merged, main contains migration commit

steps.name: Ensure clean gh auth
steps.action: In this shell: `unset GITHUB_TOKEN` then `gh auth status` must show keyring token active. Fail fast if not.

steps.name: Checkout main and tag
steps.action: `git checkout main && git pull --ff-only origin main && git tag -a v0.1.0 -m 'Release 0.1.0 — license migration to GPL-3.0-or-later' && git push origin v0.1.0`.

steps.name: Draft release notes
steps.action: Write state/release-notes.md with: headline license change, explicit callout that v0.0.11 and prior remain Apache-2.0, inbound=outbound note, version rationale (0.1.0 chosen over 1.0.0 because still pre-1.0), link to gnu.org/licenses/gpl-3.0.html, changelog of non-license changes since v0.0.11.

steps.name: Publish release
steps.action: `gh release create v0.1.0 --title 'v0.1.0 — License migration to GPL-3.0' --notes-file state/release-notes.md`. Do NOT mark as prerelease. Do NOT attach binaries (none built yet).

steps.name: Verify propagation
steps.action: `gh api repos/vburnz/mcp-gateway-firewall --jq .license.key` returns `gpl-3.0`. `gh release view v0.1.0` returns the release. Log both to state/convergence-log.md.

autonomy_directive: DO NOT ask clarifying questions. DO NOT pause for confirmation WITHIN a phase. DO pause at phase boundaries to run verification. If ambiguous, choose the most reasonable interpretation and document in state/convergence-log.md. If blocked, write BLOCKED.md with the specific blocker and halt. Hard-stop on any of: external contributor detected, GPL-incompatible dependency without mitigation, gh auth unresolvable. License publication is irreversible — treat Phase 3 verification gate as load-bearing; do not proceed from P2→P3 until all P2 criteria green.
notes_for_executor: The `--interrogate` flag chained by the user after `/execute` should apply REOPEN_SOLUTION after P2 converges but BEFORE P3 starts. That is the irreversibility gate. Questions to ask at interrogation: (a) Did I pick 0.1.0 or 1.0.0, and is that documented? (b) Is the LICENSE file verbatim GPL-3.0 with no accidental modifications? (c) Did I remember the history note for Apache-2.0? (d) Is there a CONTRIBUTING.md that needs updating?

Cargo.lock audit (S-102): if `cargo-license` is not installed, fall back to `cargo tree --prefix none | sort -u` followed by manual spot-check of the top 20 direct deps, then document assumption that transitive Apache/MIT/BSD deps are compatible.
The working tree currently has untracked `diff.diff` and `crates/arbiter-session/tests/property_tests.proptest-regressions` — DO NOT include these in the migration commit. Leave them untracked.
If `cargo check --workspace` surfaces pre-existing failures unrelated to the license change, document in state/convergence-log.md and proceed — license migration does not own those failures.
